metadata.title: Gru's v0 Gate: One Sentence or You Don't Build

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Salut, Liam — in for Bear. This is Liam, in for Bear? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: This is Liam, in for Bear?
props.segment: Gru's v0 Gate: One Sentence or

beats.narration_text: Students skip the hardest step. They open a session, type what they want to build, and start building four things at once while calling them one. Gru's slash v0 gate is a single-command refusal: give me one sentence describing one system with one user and one done condition, or I will not proceed. That unhelpfulness is the feature. This is Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: /v0 I want to build an agent to help with Unity projects.

beats.narration_text: Gru refuses. The first attempt fails three requirements simultaneously. No single system — the phrase 'help with Unity projects' contains at least five distinct systems. No named user — help whom, doing what. No done condition — what does done look like. All three failed.
beats.new_visual_element: Gru refusal message — three gates failed

beats.narration_text: Second attempt. The student tries again — now the sentence has four 'ands': an auditor, a refactor agent, a test generator, and a CLAUDE.md author. Four things. Still one sentence. The gate catches it.

Fourteen minutes. No code. Only artifact: a twenty-eight word sentence. But that sentence revealed three scope mistakes that would have wasted the first four hours of the build. The most expensive work in software is deciding what to build. Gru charges fourteen minutes up front.
beats.new_visual_element: Passing /v0 sentence — 28 words, 3 scope mistakes revealed

beats.narration_text: Brooks called it the essential complexity — deciding what to build is harder than building it, and no tool can do it for you. The gate's unhelpfulness is not a bug. It is the mechanism that forces the human to do the one thing that cannot be delegated.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — passed /v0 sentence + the essential vs accidental split

beats.narration_text: Try it before your next build. Paste this — and do not proceed until it passes.

props.command: /v0 [describe what you want to build in one sentence — one system, one named user, one done condition. If your sentence has 'and' in it, split the project first.]

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Brooks called it the essential complexity — deciding what to build is harder than building it, and no tool can do it for you. The gate's unhelpfulness is not a bug. It is the mechanism that forces the human to do the one thing that cannot be delegated. Try it before your next build. Paste this — and do not proceed until it passes.

props.artifactHeading: Gru's v0 Gate: One Sentence or You Don't
artifactLines: Brooks called it the essential complexity — deciding what to build is harder than build…
Try it before your next build.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Gru's v0 Gate: One Sentence or You Don't Build] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
props.segment: Gru's v0 Gate: One Sentence or

props.title: Gru's v0 Gate: One Sentence or You Don't Build
